Job Role Description: AWARD: Social, Community, Home Care and Disability Services Award (SCHADS) LEVEL: Social And Community Services Employee (SACS), Level 4 Position Purpose: The First Nations Community Partnerships Officer plays a vital role in strengthening and sustaining respectful relationships between SecondBite | FareShare and First Nations Communities. The role is grounded in listening, connection and trust building to ensure the Meals for the Mob program is culturally safe, community led and responsive to local needs. This position focuses on nurturing long term partnerships with First Nations organisations, Elders, leaders and agencies, supporting Communities to access nutritious meals in ways that respect Cultural protocols, priorities and ways of working. While there are operational and coordination elements, these are supported by the broader SecondBite | FareShare team. The primary emphasis of the role is engagement, collaboration and advocacy, ensuring Communities feel confident, included and empowered in their participation.
Job Role Identifier: First Nations Community Engagement Officer | SCHADS, SACS Lvl4
Job Role Skills and Experience: KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Community Relationships and Engagement • Build and maintain strong, respectful and trusting partnerships with First Nations Communities and organisations. • Act as a trusted point of contact for Community partners, listening to needs and ensuring the program adapts to local contexts. • Work alongside Elders, Community leaders, councils and organisations to co design solutions and strengthen participation in Meals for the Mob. • Support community cook ups and other engagement activities that celebrate Culture, connection and shared outcomes. • Provide clear, supportive communication to help partner agencies understand and navigate program requirements with confidence. • Advocate for Community perspectives internally to ensure voices are heard and respected in decision making. Program Support and Coordination • Work collaboratively with internal teams to ensure meal distribution and agency support are well coordinated. • Provide feedback from Communities to internal teams to support responsive operational planning, including logistics, meal sizes and seasonal access considerations. • Support the onboarding of new agencies and maintain relationships with existing partners, with administrative and operational support provided by the wider team. • Ensure agency agreements and compliance requirements are met, with guidance and support where required. • Assist in maintaining accurate records and agency information in relevant systems, with shared responsibility across the team. Cultural Safety and Organisational Development • Contribute to building Cultural awareness and understanding across SecondBite | FareShare by sharing insights and learnings from Community partnerships. • Work alongside First Nations Officers in other states and colleagues across the organisation to support cultural safety initiatives. • Actively support the organisation’s Reconciliation Action Plan (RAP) through engagement, advocacy and lived practice. • Support a culturally safe approach to risk, safety and reputation by identifying issues early and escalating concerns appropriately. Safety, Integrity and Professional Practice • Model safety first behaviours and ensure duties are performed in line with relevant legislation, policies and procedures. • Immediately report hazards, incidents or concerns and support appropriate responses. • Maintain high standards of integrity, confidentiality and respectful conduct at all times. • Contribute to a positive, inclusive and values aligned workplace culture. Other Duties • Participate in regular supervision and team meetings to support shared learning and collaboration. • Provide timely and accurate information for reporting and organisational requirements. • Undertake other duties within the scope of the role and the employee’s skills and experience as directed. POSITION COMPETENCIES: Qualifications, Skills and Experience • Strong relationship management and Community engagement skills. • Experience working with First Nations Communities is essential, experience with remote Communities is beneficial. • Confidence in navigating sensitive issues with respect, care and integrity. • Strong verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to communicate across cultures and contexts. • Ability to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team environment. Personal Attributes • Genuine commitment to cultural safety, self-determination and reconciliation. • Someone who can walk alongside others, roll with change and remain strong in spirit. • High levels of integrity, confidentiality and respect. • Ability to work independently while being well supported by a team. • Culturally aware, involved and respectful of First Nations Customs and Lore.

and religious life • Promote vocational material for the parishes, schools and agencies of the Diocese on Vocations with the support of others for Sunday, Good Shepherd Sunday and the Holy Spirit college contacts and seek appropriate opportunities to connect with students • Liaise with leaders of youth initiatives, parish team, volunteers • Develop relationships with diocesan personnel in relation to diocesan events ensuring collaboration • Actively seek external formation opportunities for the young people of the Diocese • Work with Bishop on formation programs for young people in the Diocese • Lead the men’s ministry in the Diocese Events & Resources • Plan yearly calendars for all relevant Diocesan and external events for the young people and young families of the Diocese • youth evangelisation • Develop and coordinate youth, vocations and family life resources for parish groups Other Duties • Fulfil the role of Executive Officer to the Diocesan Vocations Taskforce • Identify and suggest any personal needs for development and training • Any other duties as requested • Be willing to travel and stay away from home regularly

Job Role Description: The Career Coach (CC) plays a crucial role in empowering individuals with disability, injury, or a health condition to achieve their employment goals under the new Inclusive Employment Australia (IEA) framework. This role involves providing information, support, and representation to participants to ensure their rights are upheld and they can participate fully and equally in the IEA program and the broader community. This role assists participants throughout their employment journey from initial contact and job preparation to securing and maintaining employment. The Disability Advocate focuses on addressing both individual and systemic barriers to inclusive employment, promoting self-advocacy, and ensuring participants’ needs are met in the workplace and beyond.

Job Role Description: Faith Basis of Appointment We see the appointment of staff to Trinity Christian School as a ministry calling. The ethos, aims and objects of a Christian School are based on: > acceptance of the Lordship of Christ; and > acceptance of the Bible as the revealed Word of God Within Trinity Christian School these are expounded in the Doctrinal Basis. The Doctrinal Basis does not exhaust the extent of the beliefs of the School and its members. The Bible itself, as the inspired and infallible Word of God, that speaks with final authority concerning truth, morality, and the proper conduct of mankind, is the sole and final source of all that the School and its members believe. You must support the School in these areas and set an example of personal conduct and behaviour consistent with the School’s beliefs and ethos as outlined in documents made available to prospective employees prior to interview. Any appointment is conditional upon staff maintaining and demonstrating a continuing firm personal belief and lifestyle consistent with the School’s Doctrinal Basis and the other documents provided to staff, this being an essential condition of the appointment. A Learning Centred Christian School At Trinity Christian School we endeavour to make the link between our faith, what we do and how we teach. We call our students and school community to grow “loving hearts, enquiring minds and generous spirits”. At Trinity, how we teach must help our students in becoming who they were created to be in Christ. How we teach should also bring a Kingdom perspective, set students’ minds on things above, deliver meaning and show purpose. The pedagogy in our classrooms at Trinity should engage enquiring minds, build questioning, allow for grappling, exploring and reasoning. The students we teach should be given opportunities to grow and serve, so they may see who they are becoming and their purpose. As we undertake to grow our learners in both faith and learning, we must build in them the capacity to do so. As stated in our Strategic Priorities for 2024 – 2027, we have several key areas of focus that we are working toward: Christian Faith and Growth The spiritual nurturing of all individuals in the Christian faith, within a caring community of students, staff and families united by vision, mission and values. A passionate focus on realising the transformative power of the Gospel of Jesus, developing Biblical knowledge, and understanding and fulfilling the purpose of a Christian life. Excellence in Education and Learning All students actively engaged, growing and thriving as learners in a learning centred school. Parent partnership and student agency enhanced and supported. A commitment to educational excellence with the creation and support of new ways of learning, varied student pathways and models of education, to meet the needs of each learner. Community and Belonging A movement by all students, staff and families from joining and attending the school to belonging and actively investing in the Trinity community. Connection and relationship developed and strengthened with those in the School’s history as well as future community members. Connecting with the World and Shaping Tomorrow Ongoing design and formation of our 2030 learning environment for the community now and in the future. A growth in our reach and impact on Christian Education nationally and globally, including shaping a reputation as a centre for staffing development and excellence. Benefits of Working at Trinity Christian School Trinity Christian School is an employer that promotes staff excellence and values the benefit that our professional team bring to the school. In addition to the highly competitive remuneration and conditions as set out in the MEA we offer the following additional benefits to our staff across the school including: > Attractive salary packaging arrangements. > School fee concessions. > Generous annual Staff Development Award program. > Access to an Employee Assistance Program. > Access to annual influenza vaccinations. Other Essential Requirements Employment Conditions Employment will be subject to the conditions of the current ACT Christian School General Staff Multi-Enterprise Agreement 2021 or its replacement. The salary and classification for this position will be dependent on the qualifications and experience of the successful applicant. A Committed, Practicing Christian Applicants must be able to demonstrate a personal faith and commitment to the Lord Jesus Christ and a lifestyle consistent with that faith, including an active commitment to a Christian fellowship. Right to Work In Australia Applicants must be Australian Citizens or hold a current and valid working visa. The School will not consider sponsorship nor support a visa application process. Please do not submit an application if you cannot meet this requirement. Working With Vulnerable People Check In keeping with the School’s policy and legislative requirements, any offer of employment is subject to you providing a satisfactory Working With Vulnerable People check prior to commencing employment and maintaining this for the duration of your employment. Acknowledgement The requirements of this job description are intended to describe the general nature and responsibility of the work in this role. These statements are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks and skills required of the job. Employees will also be required to follow any other job-related instructions and school policies, and to perform other job-related duties requested by their Manager to support the School’s compliance with its legislative obligations. The Manager may, through consultation with the employee, vary the responsibilities of the position temporarily as required, but within the skills and responsibility levels appropriate to the position. Further Information All enquiries will be treated confidentially. Initial questions can be directed to the People & Culture Administrator, Katherine Boyd by phone on +61 2 6210 7500 or email employment@tcs.act.edu.au Applications are ongoing and are to be submitted electronically as a single PDF document as an attachment to the online application form. Trinity Christian School 34 McBryde Crescent Wanniassa ACT Australia 2903

Participate in capacity planning and performance monitoring of systems to ensure optimal functionality. Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users. Familiarity with remote support tools and software for effective issue resolution. Knowledge of network protocols, server environments, and virtualisation technologies. Experience with ticketing systems for tracking support requests and incidents. Required experiences A minimum of 3 years’ experience in an IT Service Desk or support role, particularly within infrastructure or cloud environments. Demonstrated experience working with Microsoft Azure, AWS or similar cloud platforms. Proven track record of managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment while maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction. Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams to implement solutions effectively. Hands-on experience in configuring and supporting Windows Server environments and Active Directory management.

Job Role Description: Functions of the Role To transport clients to and from destinations as required, using BRCG transport vehicles, or if necessary, the driver's own vehicle. Drivers are to provide any required assistance to the client either with mobility issues, entering and exiting the vehicle, or finding their way to their exact destination. Roles and Responsibilities Organisation Works within the role and responsibilities for the position and complies with all organisational policies, regulations and standards, and the Code of Conduct; Shows commitment to ongoing skill development by participating in training and development activities; Represents the organisation as the first point of contact via a range of communication methods; Demonstrates confidentiality and diversity awareness; Participates as a respectful team member contributing to a harmonious workplace; Takes on board, accommodates and accepts feedback; Maintain a professional relationship with clients and team members and possess high levels of trust, honesty, respect and integrity; Supports clients to Live Their Best Life. Duties and Tasks Take clients to and from medical appointments, shopping and business appointments as arranged by the Scheduler; Provide any required assistance to the client either with mobility issues, entering and exiting the vehicle, or finding their way to their exact destination; Where possible familiarise yourself with possible destinations such as hospitals and specialist rooms; Building positive rapport with clients; Cleaning of vehicles when required; Completion of daily vehicle inspection sheet; Meal delivery runs for RC ‘Freeze and Heat’ meals; Reporting to client's Care Partner deterioration of client's health or other issues of concern.

Demonstrated assessment and counselling experience in a cross-cultural context and experience in working with children, adolescents and adults in a clinical setting. 3. Demonstrated capacity providing clinical group treatment and demonstrated training skills. 4. Proven excellent oral, written and interpersonal communication skills and proficiency in the use of Microsoft Office. 6. Job Role Description: The Position This position works with children and young people on statutory orders in out of home care placements. The worker will establish placement supports for new placements and make referrals to appropriate support services for carers, children and families. This position is also required to write detailed assessments of families and carers, placement suitability and support needs ensuring that the plan supports connection to their Aboriginal Culture and the Community. Key Responsibilities Participate in comprehensive family assessment that identify strengths and goals with a focus on the health, wellbeing and safety needs of the children involved. Participate in the development of comprehensive family plans using a wrap-around model of support and facilitate ongoing reviews/ evaluation of family plans. Refer children and/or families to appropriate services based on needs and goals, work in conjunction with services to ensure that referrals are responded to in a way that acknowledges the unique needs of each referral and situation. Complete comprehensive case notes and reports such as: reports to child protection, court reports, Cultural Support Plans, quarterly reports, LAC documentation, CIMS reporting and Data collection as required maintain records using the relevant client information systems and keep paper files in line with program requirements. Actively participate in teamwork in a manner that ensures a co-ordinated approach to service delivery and participate in professional development and training. Lead and attend meetings to support the case management of families and advocate for children and families as required. Facilitate supervised contact between children and their families. Travel to meet with families and some overnight and interstate travel may be required. Other duties as directed by your Team Leader/ Program Manager.

About the Position The Public Intoxication Response (PIR) service plays a crucial role in addressing the health and wellbeing needs of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander individuals who are intoxicated in the Greater Bendigo/Castlemaine areas, aligning with the recent decriminalisation of public intoxication by the Victorian Government. The PIR Team Leader will hold a supporting position in this service, ensuring that the program operates effectively and efficiently. The core function of the PIR team is to provide an outreach-based response to individuals in need. This response includes conducting thorough health assessments, offering safe transportation to an appropriate location, monitoring the health and overall well-being of the individual, and delivering follow-up care as needed. In essence, the PIR service operates as a critical lifeline for those who are intoxicated, ensuring their safety and well-being while also contributing to a more compassionate and health-focused approach in the context of public intoxication. Key Responsibilities The Public Intoxication Response (PIR) Team Leader will have the following responsibilities: Contribution to PIR Model Development and Evaluation: This entails actively participating in the development of the PIR model, evaluating its effectiveness, and making necessary adjustments to ensure it aligns with the evolving needs of Community. Recruitment and Management: The PIR Team Leader will support the Program Manager in recruitment and day-to-day management of the PIR team, ensuring that they are well-trained, motivated, and capable of carrying out their roles effectively. Liaison With Other Internal BDAC Programs: Work with other areas of BDAC to facilitate referral pathways while integrating with the broader Health Wellbeing Team. Staff Rostering, Including On-Call Scheduling: Organising and managing staff rosters, which include on-call schedules to ensure that there is adequate coverage and response readiness. Client Wellbeing Oversight: The PIR Team Leader will provide oversight, offering guidance and support to the team in handling complex cases, ensuring adherence to protocols, and maintaining high standards of care. Reporting and Evaluation: The Team Leader will be responsible for collecting data, preparing reports, and evaluating the programs performance. Client Advocacy: The PIR Team Leader advocates for the rights and well-being of clients, ensuring that they receive appropriate care and support while navigating the complexities of the program Community and Stakeholder Engagement: Working with the Program Manager to build meaningful stakeholder relationships that will benefit Community using the service.

Position Objective The Chronic Care Coordinator will: Ensure that patients are accessing services consistent with their GP Care Plan, including provision of appropriate clinical care, arranging the services required and ensuring regular reviews are undertaken by the patients primary care providers. Ensure a patients access to an urgent and essential health or specialist services, including certain medical aids or the necessary transport to access the series, where this is not publicly available. Key Position Responsibilities Primary Responsibilities Review eligibility under the Care Coordinator Supplementary Services (CCSS). Aboriginal Patients must be enrolled for chronic disease management in a medical service and have a current care plan. Ensure that patients are accessing services consistent with their GP Management Plan including clinical care arranging follow-ups and regular reviews of the plan are undertaken Ensure that patients are able to access specialist appointments through providing appropriate care and purchase of medical aids. Ensure that priority is given to patients most in need of care coordination services to improve health outcomes. Work closely with Murray PHN and the Aboriginal Health Project Manager for guidance around clinical governance, risk management and reporting on use of supplementary services funding. Promote and educate the local Aboriginal community to explain the role of the Chronic Care Coordinator and how the service can be accessed Build and maintain networks with relevant service providers and agencies. Administration Compliance Model and abide by BDAC Values, Code of Conduct and Policy and Procedures; Participate actively in and facilitate supervision and professional development activities; Ensure that you participate in team meetings, staff meetings and other community activities as requested; Ensure that you adhere to legislative requirements; Ensure that you report any risks identified immediately to your line manager; Participate within the team to ensure performance against expectations including performance management and staff development, in accordance with BDACs policies and procedures; Ensure that all staff are provided with and operate in a safe environment in accordance with BDACS OHS policies and procedures; Participate in Continuous Quality Improvement (CQI) activities.

Job Role Description: Job Summary The Carnivals Co-ordinator will oversee the operation of Struddys carnivals, with a focus on our major contracts (SSA & QRSS). They will be responsible for ordering merchandise, event management, and managing carnival staff. Specifically, they will organise logistics, including set-up and pack-down of events and ensuring necessary equipment is ordered, manage staff hours/carnival roster, and ensure merchandise for carnivals is ordered in advance. They will ensure that Struddys merchandise tents adhere to Work Health and Safety standards, and that carnival staff are following all company policies and procedures. Responsibilities and Duties Required to work flexible hours during carnival season, including weekend work and overtime. Keep Struddys merchandise tents clean and tidy at all times. Responsible for carnival event management, including set-up and pack-down of events, ensuring necessary equipment is ordered, and managing carnival staff on-site. Manage staff working at events, ensuring they clock in-and-out correctly, are following the roster and taking breaks as instructed. Ensure merchandise is ordered for each event in advance. Organise staffing for calendared events in advance. Ensure that Struddys merchandise tents adhere to Work Health and Safety standards. Ensure carnivals staff adhere to all company policies and procedures. Report on the results of each carnival to upper management, including total sales, stock turnover, and staff efficiency.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Valid driver’s licence Excellent organisational skills Proven leadership skills Able to work under pressure Strong work ethic Excellent communication skills

Job Role Description: The Career Coach (CC) plays a crucial role in empowering individuals with disability, injury, or a health condition to achieve their employment goals under the new Inclusive Employment Australia (IEA) framework. This role involves providing information, support, and representation to participants to ensure their rights are upheld and they can participate fully and equally in the IEA program and the broader community. This role assists participants throughout their employment journey from initial contact and job preparation to securing and maintaining employment. The Disability Advocate focuses on addressing both individual and systemic barriers to inclusive employment, promoting self-advocacy, and ensuring participants’ needs are met in the workplace and beyond.

Faith Basis of Appointment We see the appointment of staff to Trinity Christian School as a ministry calling. The ethos, aims and objects of a Christian School are based on: > acceptance of the Lordship of Christ; and > acceptance of the Bible as the revealed Word of God Within Trinity Christian School these are expounded in the Doctrinal Basis. The Doctrinal Basis does not exhaust the extent of the beliefs of the School and its members. The Bible itself, as the inspired and infallible Word of God, that speaks with final authority concerning truth, morality, and the proper conduct of mankind, is the sole and final source of all that the School and its members believe. You must support the School in these areas and set an example of personal conduct and behaviour consistent with the School’s beliefs and ethos as outlined in documents made available to prospective employees prior to interview. Any appointment is conditional upon staff maintaining and demonstrating a continuing firm personal belief and lifestyle consistent with the School’s Doctrinal Basis and the other documents provided to staff, this being an essential condition of the appointment. A Learning Centred Christian School At Trinity Christian School we endeavour to make the link between our faith, what we do and how we teach. We call our students and school community to grow “loving hearts, enquiring minds and generous spirits”. At Trinity, how we teach must help our students in becoming who they were created to be in Christ. How we teach should also bring a Kingdom perspective, set students’ minds on things above, deliver meaning and show purpose. The pedagogy in our classrooms at Trinity should engage enquiring minds, build questioning, allow for grappling, exploring and reasoning. The students we teach should be given opportunities to grow and serve, so they may see who they are becoming and their purpose. As we undertake to grow our learners in both faith and learning, we must build in them the capacity to do so. As stated in our Strategic Priorities for 2024 – 2027, we have several key areas of focus that we are working toward: Christian Faith and Growth The spiritual nurturing of all individuals in the Christian faith, within a caring community of students, staff and families united by vision, mission and values. A passionate focus on realising the transformative power of the Gospel of Jesus, developing Biblical knowledge, and understanding and fulfilling the purpose of a Christian life. Excellence in Education and Learning All students actively engaged, growing and thriving as learners in a learning centred school. Parent partnership and student agency enhanced and supported. A commitment to educational excellence with the creation and support of new ways of learning, varied student pathways and models of education, to meet the needs of each learner. Community and Belonging A movement by all students, staff and families from joining and attending the school to belonging and actively investing in the Trinity community. Connection and relationship developed and strengthened with those in the School’s history as well as future community members. Connecting with the World and Shaping Tomorrow Ongoing design and formation of our 2030 learning environment for the community now and in the future. A growth in our reach and impact on Christian Education nationally and globally, including shaping a reputation as a centre for staffing development and excellence. Benefits of Working at Trinity Christian School Trinity Christian School is an employer that promotes staff excellence and values the benefit that our professional team bring to the school. In addition to the highly competitive remuneration and conditions as set out in the MEA we offer the following additional benefits to our staff across the school including: > Attractive salary packaging arrangements. > School fee concessions. > Generous annual Staff Development Award program. > Access to an Employee Assistance Program. > Access to annual influenza vaccinations. Other Essential Requirements Employment Conditions Employment will be subject to the conditions of the current ACT Christian School General Staff Multi-Enterprise Agreement 2021 or its replacement. The salary and classification for this position will be dependent on the qualifications and experience of the successful applicant. A Committed, Practicing Christian Applicants must be able to demonstrate a personal faith and commitment to the Lord Jesus Christ and a lifestyle consistent with that faith, including an active commitment to a Christian fellowship. Right to Work In Australia Applicants must be Australian Citizens or hold a current and valid working visa. The School will not consider sponsorship nor support a visa application process. Please do not submit an application if you cannot meet this requirement. Working With Vulnerable People Check In keeping with the School’s policy and legislative requirements, any offer of employment is subject to you providing a satisfactory Working With Vulnerable People check prior to commencing employment and maintaining this for the duration of your employment. These statements are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks and skills required of the job. Employees will also be required to follow any other job-related instructions and school policies, and to perform other job-related duties requested by their Manager to support the School’s compliance with its legislative obligations. The Manager may, through consultation with the employee, vary the responsibilities of the position temporarily as required, but within the skills and responsibility levels appropriate to the position. About the Position As a member of the Finance Team, the Finance Officer is primarily responsible for supporting BDACs operations in managing the day-to-day financial operations of BDAC and will undertake a wide range of finance related tasks to achieve this. The role will be supported by the Financial Accountant, and involves managing financial records, processing transactions, and assisting with financial reporting and reconciliations. The Finance Officer plays a vital role in ensuring the efficient operation of the Finance Team by maintaining accurate records and supporting financial processes. Key Responsibilities Processing accounts payable and receivable transactions, ensuring timely and accurate payments and collections. Assist with payroll processing and reporting, including verifying pay amounts, hours of work, deductions, and the payment and lodgement of superannuation. Reconciling bank statements and financial transactions, identifying and resolving discrepancies. Complying with and assisting in enforcing financial policies and procedures, including the delegation of approval for payments. This includes maintaining internal controls by monitoring financial processes and reporting any irregularities Assist in the preparation, maintenance, and processing of financial documents, including invoices, receipts, credit cards, purchase orders, and contracts. Handle correspondence related to financial matters, including responding to enquiries and communicating with vendors, clients, and internal teams. Support the finance team in preparing for audits, budgets and reporting as directed. Perform general administrative finance duties, including the management petty cash, essential cards, filing, and photocopying. Other additional duties as necessary to ensure the smooth operation of the finance team.

Job Role Title: Aboriginal Men's Hub Practitioner - The Orange Door
Job Role Description: The Position This is an exciting new role based in the Aboriginal Response Team in the Loddon Orange Door. The successful applicant will work with Aboriginal men and younger males, as well as fathers of Aboriginal children, coming through The Orange Door (TOD) for support around family violence and child wellbeing. The role will work to support men to engage with services, including working collaboratively to transition men into programs run through BDAC, most commonly with the mens practitioners in the Family Safety and Healing Program, as well as Family Services, AOD and SEWB, amongst others. The successful applicant will develop strong relationships across the service system in the Dja Dja Wurrung region to support the best service connection for men coming through TOD. Key Responsibilities Providing culturally safe and responsive support to Aboriginal men, young males and fathers/carers of Aboriginal children by: Co-working and providing daily specialist practice support for the Hubs staff Managing a caseload of complex and/or sensitive cases Operating with autonomy and accountability in supporting Aboriginal men, young people and fathers/carers of Aboriginal children. Working directly with the Mens Practitioners in the BDAC Family Safety and Healing Program to promote effective transition from TOD to ongoing support for people who use violence (PUV), young males and fathers of Aboriginal Children needing family support. Mentoring and developing Hub Staff in working with Aboriginal men, young males and fathers/carers of Aboriginal children: Building capability of Hub practitioners to deliver culturally competent responses to Aboriginal children, women and men, informed by cultural expertise, client experience and in line with the relevant practice frameworks and legislation Providing practitioners with relevant information and access to systems to support safe and effective responses to Aboriginal children and families as part of an integrated practice approach Modelling integrated practice approaches and behaviours integral to ethical clinical practice working with Aboriginal children and families Sharing practice knowledge on Aboriginal approaches to holistic healing and whole of family practices Building the cultural safety of the Hub and supporting choice and self-determination of Aboriginal people. Liaising with organisations and services within the Hub network in order to improve direct service issues for Aboriginal people accessing services through the Hub. Keep accurate and complete records of your work activities in accordance with legislative requirements and the Victoria Governments, information security and privacy policies and requirements. Take reasonable care for your own health and safety and for that of others in the workplace by working in accordance with legislative requirements and occupational health and safety (OHS) policies and procedures. Other duties as directed by the Aboriginal Practice Leader and consistent with the overall goals and responsibilities of this position
Job Role Identifier: Aboriginal Men's Hub Practitioner - The Orange Door

Job Role Description: Performing advanced installation, commissioning, calibration and servicing of CST equipment, including weighers, IntelliRoll systems and associated components; Leading complex troubleshooting and fault-finding on CST and third-party weighing and process control systems; Providing expert-level diagnosis and resolution of technical issues across a wide range of belt weighing and instrumentation systems; Attending high-priority, complex or critical service jobs, including remote, national and international site work; Training, mentoring and developing Service Technicians, including hands-on training and technical guidance both on-site and remotely; Supporting the development of technician capability, including assisting with competency frameworks and trade-related skills (e.g. weigher certifications); Acting as a technical authority within the service team, providing guidance on best practices, troubleshooting and system optimisation; Liaising directly with customers to resolve technical issues and provide high-level support and advice; Working with a variety of third-party weighing systems and equipment, applying broad industry knowledge to diagnose and resolve issues; Supporting onboarding and technical development of new technicians; Assisting in the development and improvement of service procedures, training materials and technical documentation; Completing detailed service reports, documentation and job records to a high standard; Providing feedback to engineering and R&D teams on product performance, recurring issues and improvement opportunities; Ensuring all work is carried out in accordance with QA procedures, safety standards and site requirements; Maintaining tools, equipment and vehicles in a safe and operational condition; Identifying and raising Opportunities for Improvement (OFIs) across service delivery and product performance. Authority Lead technical execution on complex or critical service jobs; Provide direction and technical guidance to Service Technicians; Represent CST as a technical expert in customer interactions; Escalate and support resolution of complex technical issues; Contribute to training, standards and technical development; Raise Opportunities for Improvement (OFIs).
Job Role Skills and Experience: Extensive experience as a Service Technician within weighing, instrumentation or process control environments; Strong expertise in belt weighers and related systems, including CST and third-party equipment; Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot complex mechanical, electrical and system-level issues; Experience working across multiple OEM systems and technologies (highly regarded); Proven ability to train, mentor and support other technicians; Strong understanding of mining and industrial site environments; Ability to work independently on complex and high-risk jobs; Excellent communication and customer-facing skills; Strong commitment to safety and quality; Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and documentation; Weigher trade certification or equivalent industry-recognised qualification (highly desirable); Relevant trade or technical qualification (Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation or similar); Willingness and ability to travel nationally and internationally; Valid driver’s licence; Australian work rights.

Job Role Description: A Principal is responsible for managing a school or educational institution. They develop and implement academic and disciplinary policies, supervise and evaluate teaching staff, oversee student programs, and manage school budgets. Principals must be experienced educators with excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills. They should be able to create a positive learning environment and promote a culture of excellence among students and staff.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Leadership: Demonstrated ability to lead and manage a team of teachers and staff, set goals and priorities, and create a positive school culture. Communication: Excellent communication skills to effectively communicate with students, parents, teachers, and other stakeholders. Curriculum and Instruction: In-depth understanding of curriculum and instruction in subject/grade level, as well as the ability to stay up-to-date with new trends and best practices. School Administration: Knowledge of school administration, including budgeting, hiring, and policy development. Decision-making: The ability to make sound decisions based on data and analysis. Problem-solving: The ability to identify and solve complex problems that may arise in the school. Interpersonal skills: The ability to build relationships and work collaboratively with teachers, staff, parents, and community members. Education and Certification: Masters degree in education or a related field, relevant teaching and administrative experience, and state-issued administrative certification. Knowledge of Laws and Regulations: Knowledge of federal and state laws and regulations related to education, including student safety, teacher certification, and testing requirements. Visionary: A visionary leader who can motivate and inspire their team and develop a clear vision for the schools future.

Job Role Description: Position Purpose The Sales Representative is responsible for driving profitable revenue growth through the sale of new and used agricultural machinery, attachments, finance products and precision agriculture solutions. This role builds long term customer relationships, actively manages a defined sales territory and positions integrated machinery and technology solutions that improve customer productivity and profitability. Success requires disciplined territory management, strong commercial acumen and consistent execution. Key Responsibilities 1. Sales & Revenue Growth Achieve or exceed annual whole goods sales targets. Maintain structured pipeline management and accurate forecasting. Identify and convert new business opportunities. Promote bundled solutions including machinery, attachments, finance, service agreements and technology. Responsible for presentation of all Saleable New and Used Machinery within the Branch, in conjunction with your team, to ensure machines are always displayed appropriately and ready for sale and display 2. Customer Relationship Management Conduct regular on farm visits within allocated territory. Plan and prospect your allocated territory including cold calling and introduction of new opportunities Develop structured call plans and customer contact schedules. Deliver professional demonstrations and presentations. Ensure post sale follow up and high levels of customer satisfaction. 3. Precision Agriculture & Technology Integration Promote John Deere Precision Ag solutions and digital platforms. Demonstrate agronomic and economic value through data driven insights. Support customers with Operations Centre adoption. Collaborate with Precision Ag specialists where required. 4. Product & Market Expertise Maintain up to date knowledge of all saleable equipment. Monitor competitor activity and market trends. Provide regular market intelligence to leadership. 5. Internal Collaboration Work closely with Service, Parts and Administration teams. Job Role Description: The Diocesan Professional Standards and Safeguarding Manager will manage the operations of the Professional Standards Office and be responsible to the Bishop for the ongoing management of old and new cases within the Diocese. The role will foster and enhance a culture that encourages cultural safety for children, young people, and adults-at-risk in compliance with the National Catholic Safeguarding Standards. The role will support the local Parish Safeguarding Representatives (PSRs) and have overall responsibility for the ongoing management of safeguarding complaints and professional standards cases. The Diocesan Professional Standards and Safeguarding Office will ensure compliance with the Safeguarding Policy across all the Diocesan entities and in all parishes. This will be achieved through the provision of induction and education resources and setting up and maintenance of recording systems for parishes and quality control of induction and training. Key Responsibilities Professional Standards • Support and advise the Bishop in the management of boundary violation cases. • Implement and continue to reflect on the outcomes of the recommendations of the Royal Commission into Institutional Responses to Child Sexual Abuse and consider the implications for the Catholic Diocese of Rockhampton. • Provide timely, contemporaneous, professional advice to the Bishop on all matters relating to professional standards and the Diocese’s Code of Conduct. • Advise on compliance with the National Response Protocol. • Conduct Professional Standards processes at the request of the Bishop. • Connect with the Director of the Professional Standards Office of Queensland on professional standards matters. • Receive requests and professional standards declarations of visiting clergy, religious and lay people, advise the Bishop, and record and issue approval letters. • Manage the Australian Catholic Ministry Register (ACMR) for clergy. • Advise the Bishop of expiry of Blue Card, Police Checks and ACMR statuses. • Liaise with statutory authorities on professional standards and safeguarding matters as required/mandated. • Receive complaints/concerns from within and without of the Diocese and act in accordance with procedures in an empathic and trauma-informed manner. • Establish and manage the Diocesan response to survivors of abuse and manage the support process for respondents, including pastoral support and the establishment and of Risk Management Plans and implementation of relevant supports. • Complete professional standards risk assessments as required. • Complete and manage Safety Plans as required. • Keep timely, accurate and confidential notes. • Provide information to other church entities and Australian Catholic Safeguarding Ltd (ACSL) as required. • Provide information to diocesan insurers as directed by the Director of Diocesan Services. • Respond to queries from within and outside the Church on professional standards and safeguarding relevant matters. • Serve as the Executive Officer, and advise and collaborate with the Bishop’s Professional Standards Committee and the Bishop about appropriate management of all abuse allegations, claims or concerns, including effective communication. • Ensure integration between the Risk Management Committee and the Safeguarding Committee by participating on the Risk Management Committee. • Support/advise and attend facilitation and apology sessions as required by the Bishop. • Manage participation in the National Redress Scheme in collaboration with legal advisors. • Provide accurate and timely evidence, reports and research to Diocesan solicitors. • Attend regular meetings with Director of Professional Standards Office Catholic Church Queensland. • Attend regular meetings with Catholic Education Student Protection lead and CatholicCare Central Queensland Safeguarding lead. • Establish and manage the Diocesan response to survivors of abuse and manage the support process for respondents, including pastoral support and the establishment and of Risk Management Plans and implementation of relevant supports. • Monitor and take calls made to the Professional Standards 1800 number during business hours, checking occasionally on weekends. • Be available to respond to and support the Bishop through a professional standards crisis outside of business hours, if required. Safeguarding • Advise the Bishop on all matters applying to safeguarding within the Diocese. • Ensure safeguarding policies and procedures are compliant with National Catholic Safeguarding Standards and write/update policies and procedures as necessary. • Ensure parishes are compliant with policies and procedures through internal audit. • Ensure every parish has a formally appointed and inducted PSR. • Support PSRs in their roles, including by coordinating group PSR meetings. • Ensure that safeguarding training is provided as per the NCSS and Diocese requirements. • Maintain a training database. • Review and update safeguarding training in light of NCSS, legislation and feedback. • Oversee the volunteer recruitment process and recording of this information. • Manage the safeguarding risks contained on the risk matrix and attend Diocesan and agency Risk Management Committee meetings on request. • Attend provincial safeguarding meetings and host annual provincial safeguarding meetings. • Provide tailored training to clergy, religious, and seminarians as required. Reporting & Supervision • Provide regular briefings and written monthly management reports to the Bishop. • Engage in external professional supervision for case management matters as approved by the line manager. • Engage in managerial supervision and appraisal. • Engage in spiritual supervision for personal well-being as approved by the line manager. • Respond to reporting requests from the Australian Catholic Bishops’ Conference (ACBC) and ACSL. Safeguarding & Professional Standards Office • Manage the operations of the Professional Standards Office. • Supervise the Safeguarding Administration Officer, if applicable, ensuring the timely and accurate processing of permission applications, Working with Children Checks, Australian Catholic Migrant & Refugee applications, and the provision of pastoral support.
Job Role Skills and Experience: • Tertiary qualifications in justice, social work, child protection or education. • Demonstrated knowledge of child protection legislation and human rights. • High level communication skills both written and verbal. • Ability to set up and interrogate databases. • Sensitivity to cultural differences. • Knowledge of trauma-informed care approaches. • Undertake travel throughout the Diocese as required to fulfil the requirements of the position. • Work flexibly including outside of office hours to meet the requirements of the role. • As an employee of the Catholic Diocese of Rockhampton, you must not at any time during or subsequent to your period of employment, disclose any information concerning the business affairs, or property of the Catholic Diocese of Rockhampton to any third party unless pre-approved by the Bishop or required by law. • Active participant in the Catholic Church community and/or lifestyle compatible with the values and teachings of the Church. • Thorough knowledge of and commitment to the Diocesan Vision Statement and Values. • Knowledge of and commitment to Catholic Social Teachings. • Ability to provide a clear criminal history record check and obtain a Queensland Working with Children (Blue Card) Check. • Ensure ongoing disclosure of any criminal charges or allegations (current or historic), as soon as possible after such charges come to the employee’s attention and in accordance with the Personnel Screening Policy as amended from time to time. Failure to comply with this ongoing obligation to disclose may lead to disciplinary action, including dismissal. • Current motor vehicle driver’s licence. Desirable • Understanding of Catholic Church hierarchy and structures. • Demonstrated ability to set up training systems. • Demonstrated ability to manage a system of policies and procedures. • Demonstrated ability to establish and maintain an internal audit system.

Job Role Title: Director of Child Safety and Cultural Support
Job Role Description: Position Objective The Director of Child Safety and Cultural Support is responsible for the service delivery of BDACs Child Safety and Cultural Support programs within the Family and Community Services unit. This role is accountable for the Mutjang bupuwingarrak mukman (A Dja Dja Wurrung name meaning keeping our kids safe) program, child safety programs, case contracting and cultural programs and the Aboriginal Child Specialist Advice and Support Service (ACSASS). This role is a member of BDACs leadership Team. Key Position Responsibilities Lead and manage BDACs Child Safety and Cultural Support programs within the Family and Community Services unit Oversee and lead the Mutjang bupuwingarrak mukman program, child safety programs, case contracting and cultural programs and the Aboriginal Child Specialist Advice and Support Service (ACSASS)team to ensure that service delivery and funding agreements are being achieved. Foster a culture of care, accountability and responsibility amongst the team, focused on delivering agreed targets and objectives in a timely manner, while placing community care at the centre of all decisions Provide strategic and operational advice and support to the Child Safety and Cultural Support management team and BDAC senior management to ensure the effective delivery of agreed programs. Work collaboratively with the Executive Director and Director Families, Healing and Response to ensure the seamless delivery of programs that may intersect or overlap across the Family and Community Services unit. Conduct regular audits on program requirements and program budgets. Report findings to the Executive Director. Provide high level advice and guidance to the Mutjang bupuwingarrak mukman team based on the Aboriginal Children placement principles and the Childrens Youth and Families Act 2005. Work with the Executive Director to identify and review BDACs programs and provide recommendations to the CEO on future expansion services for the community. Identify any programs that may need additional support by providing recommendations to the Executive Director. Prepare or contribute to funding submissions to deliver new initiatives focused on improving outcomes for community. Seek additional funding to address gaps in the operations area by advocating on behalf of the Aboriginal community needs Ensure data collection and funding reports are completed to a high standard and in a timely manner. Provide management support including performance management and supervision of employees to ensure a positive working environment. Promote BDAC programs and services to relevant external agencies and key stakeholders including participating in the relevant committees and meetings. Ensure effective ongoing liaison with partner agencies, key stakeholders, services and personnel.
Job Role Identifier: Director of Child Safety and Cultural Support

Job Role Title: Aboriginal Children in Aboriginal Care Senior Case Manager - Wartaka
Job Role Description: About the Position Reporting to the Team Leader, and Program Manager the Senior Case Manager position will lead investigations and make assessment if a child needs protection. If the child is found to need protection the Senior Case Manager will work directly with children, families, and community to ensure the childs safety and wellbeing. Where this is unable to be achieved voluntarily, it may include initiating legal proceedings and all work associated with making a protection application. Key Responsibilities Supported by the Team Leader and senior program staff, the Senior Case Manager will: Lead investigations into reports of harm received by child protection about Aboriginal children and young people, including interviews with children and families and documentation of investigation. Conduct outreach visits to family homes and manage conflicts as they arise. Make assessment and recommendations if the child or young person needs protection. Engage families on a voluntary basis, working with children, families, and community to ensure the childs safety and wellbeing, working from a strengths approach. Provide case management, including ongoing assessment and leading a care team. Refer children and or families to appropriate services, work with services to ensure that referrals are responded to in a way that acknowledges the needs. Complete written records and reports using the Client Relationship Information System and keep files in line with program requirements. Actively participate in teamwork in a manner that ensures a co-ordinated approach to service delivery. Lead and attend meetings to support the work with families and advocate for children and families as required. Make applications, prepare documentation, give evidence and make recommendations to the Childrens Court on decisions and actions in the best interests of the child. Travel to meet with families and for training, some overnight and interstate travel may be required.
Job Role Identifier: Aboriginal Children in Aboriginal Care Senior Case Manager - Wartaka

Job Role Title: Aboriginal Children in Aboriginal Care Team Leader - Yarran
Job Role Description: The Team Leader will lead a team of five staff within the Mutjang bupuwingarrak mukman Program (a Dja Dja Wurrung name meaning keeping our kids safe) The position will have oversite of 36 children authorised to BDAC under section 18 of the Childrens, Youth and Families Act 2005. The team leader will hold the responsibility to ensure administration of the Children Youth and Families Act as well as adherence to other relevant policies and procedures. This position will at times require after hours work and will take part in a rostered on-call service. KEY POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES PrimaryResponsibilities Lead and supervise a team of staff working from a strengths approach. Exercise a wide range of authorities and legal delegations under section 18 of the Children, Youth and Families Act 2005 and other specific delegations and functions including adherence to relevant practice standards. Establish and maintain effective working relationships, ongoing communication and successful negotiations with all key stakeholders both internal and external. Monitor and review key performance indicators to ensure effective service delivery with a focus on continual improvement. Use and coach staff to use a range of IT/web-based applications to manage workflow, approvals and collection of information in accordance with guidelines and privacy principles. Prepare and oversee the preparation of documentation, present information and make recommendations to the Childrens Court of Victoria and other relevant courts and tribunal processes on decisions and actions in the best interests of the child. Be clear about accountability and responsibility for decisions and involve partners in joint problem solving. Contribute the body of practice and the development of policies and procedures. Maintain the operational level of program development and service delivery. Ensure applications are made to ensure all protection orders are current and updated (if required) prior to expiry date. Protection Orders that are close to expiry must be reported to the Program Manager. Job Role Description: FUNCTIONS OF THE ROLE The Administration & Facilities Manager is responsible for overseeing the organisation’s administrative operations and facilities management to ensure the effective, safe, and reliable delivery of services. This role provides leadership and coordination of office administration, systems, and infrastructure, as well as the maintenance function responsible for supporting client-facing services and organisational assets. The position ensures facilities, environments, and operational supports are fit for purpose, compliant, and aligned with organisational needs, enabling frontline teams to deliver high‑quality services to clients. ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Organisation Works within the role and responsibilities for the position and complies with all organisational policies, regulations and standards, and Code of Conduct; Maintains professional and technical knowledge by participating in training and development activities; Represents the organisation as the first point of contact via a range of communication methods; Contributes effectively to team effort by accomplishing related results as needed; Always maintains a high level of professionalism. Duties and Tasks -Administration Oversees the administration support to a variety of areas within the organisation; When necessary, performs reception duties, including telephone enquiries, welcoming visitors and conducting frontline communications in a professional and courteous manner; Assists with booking venues and meeting rooms (internal and external) and receiving visitors to the office; Oversees the development and production of the newsletter, InfoCast's, flyers, posters, and other media; Monitors shared inbox and deals with incoming mail accordingly; Coordinates events management and catering for in-house and external events; Conducts financial transactions for clients as directed including electronic payments and receipt of cash transactions; Orders stationary and supplies as required; Undertakes routine administration duties including: Mail collection and distribution; Word processing and spreadsheet activities; Photocopying and scanning; Ensuring a professionally presented office; Provide relief at other sites when required; Records maintenance and archiving; Ensures the Administration Team keeps the bathrooms and kitchens stocked and in order. Ensures discretion and confidentiality of information/documents is maintained at all times; Assists with mailouts and other company correspondence. Duties and Tasks – Facilities Oversees the organisation’s facilities and maintenance services to ensure environments are safe, functional, compliant, and fit for purpose for clients, team members, and visitors. Manages and supervises Maintenance Officers, providing direction, support, performance oversight, and prioritisation of work activities. Coordinates and manages maintenance services that directly support client ‑ facing service delivery, including service sites, client environments, and organisational assets. Ensures timely response to maintenance requests and issues across service locations, prioritising matters that impact client safety, dignity, and continuity of services. Liaises with external contractors and service providers to coordinate repairs, servicing, and maintenance works, ensuring quality outcomes and value for money. Oversees planned and reactive maintenance programs to maintain asset condition and minimise disruption to services provided to clients. Monitors facilities and maintenance compliance with relevant legislation, safety standards, and organisational policies and procedures. Works collaboratively with service teams to ensure facilities and maintenance activities align with client needs and service delivery requirements. Maintains accurate records of maintenance activities, inspections, servicing schedules, and contractor documentation. Assists with facilities planning and minor projects, including office moves, site set ‑ ups, refurbishments, and equipment replacement. Supports incident management processes by addressing facilities or maintenance issues arising from incidents, hazards, audits, or client feedback. Promotes a proactive approach to facilities and maintenance that supports safety, service quality, and positive client experiences. Team Members’ Supervision Provides support, guidance, and direction to the teams; Ensures that all team members demonstrate appropriate behaviours when working with clients, colleagues, volunteers, and the public; Leads and promotes effective teamwork and communication between all team members; Identifies and implements team members’ training and development needs; Regularly reviews and maintains effective performance management processes; Builds workforce capability by providing ongoing informal and formal performance development and performance management to direct reports; Works alongside the team, setting the example with a proactive attitude and strong leadership skills; Oversees the recruitment, selection, orientation and on-boarding procedures of team members; Achieves results by communicating position roles, responsibilities, accountabilities and expectations to team members; Performs coaching, counselling and disciplinary procedures for team members with the support of the Head of Operations. Other Ensures a strong customer service focus through responsive, effective customer engagement; Contributes effectively to organisational events and activities. KEY RELATIONSHIPS Head of Operations Administration Team Facilities Team Management team Team Members, Clients and Stakeholders

Job Role Description: About the Position The Wurrekangek (Aboriginal Family Preservation and Reunification Response) program is funded by the Victorian Department of Families, Fairness and Housing (DFFH). In collaboration with VACCA, the Wurrekangek Program delivers an immediate, intensive, and culturally appropriate response to Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander families who are at risk of having their children removed from their care or are in the Reunification phase of their involvement with Child Protection. The core values of the program include supporting self-determination, self-advocacy and strengthening families through evidence based and culturally safe practice. Key Responsibilities The Wurrekangek (AFPR Response) Practitioner will engage with Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander families in a culturally safe and competent way. This includes engaging in regular consultation with the programs Team Leader/ Cultural Advisor and ongoing training. The Wurrekangek (AFPR Response) Practitioner will: Demonstrate ability to work with Aboriginal children, young people, and families with complex trauma needs. Have sound understanding of the history of colonisation and resulting impacts on Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander families. Work with families to ensure their cultural needs are identified early and embedded in practice. Work alongside of families to identify their strengths, barriers and needs. Implement a holistic case planning model using the cultural and evidence informed AFPR practice elements. Maintain clear and concise documentation of case progression and implementation of evidence-based interventions. Participate in all training, supervision, and consultation activities. Build strong relationships with community stakeholders and other culturally appropriate organisations.

Assisting to implement and manage cyber security policies, procedures, and standards to ensure preventive, detective, and recovery strategies are designed and working as expected to minimise risks from security threats. Conducting design and operational effectiveness assessments on the processes surrounding backups and recovery procedures. Evaluating and assessing the cyber security posture of third-party vendors and service providers to mitigate potential risks to the organisation. Collaborating with internal stakeholders to incorporate cyber security requirements into practices, procedures, and agreements. Continuously reviewing and improving cyber security processes, controls, and procedures to adapt to evolving threats and technologies. Preparing for and supporting internal and external cyber security audits and assessments. Promoting a culture of security consciousness across the organisation. Staying abreast of the latest cyber security trends, threats, and technologies to inform decision-making and keep the organisation updated on best practices.
Job Role Skills and Experience: You have: 5 years IT experience across various areas. Previous experience within Information security/Cyber GRC function. Highly favourable: CISA, CRISC, or relevant tertiary qualification. Experience in banking or with CPS 234 (APRA) obligations. Experience with other regulatory and legislative obligations such as PCI-DSS, ISO27000, Privacy, etc. Experience working within NIST CSF. Demonstrated experience or skills in: Experience in testing the design and effectiveness of security controls. A strong understanding of regulatory requirements, standards, and industry frameworks. A strong understanding of IT Governance and Cyber risk management. Experience in conducting risk assessments and participating in audit activities. Broad working knowledge of security domains. Broad understanding of technologies, infrastructure, networking, cloud, security, risk management, compliance. Experience with vulnerability management tools, SIEM, DLP, IAM
